title: Pep Hit Secondary Review Queue
description: A risk-and-compliance control workflow that sequences screening, escalation, and approval gates before transaction progression. Focus case: pep hit secondary review queue.
assumptions: Each alert_case is processed by a single analyst at a time; analyst availability is modelled via binary tokens.
Resources (evidence, watchlist items, check templates) are exclusive while allocated and must be released before reuse.
Approver and specialist availability is represented as discrete tokens and deterministic: there is no probabilistic delay model.
Escalation and approval outcomes are atomic state changes; human decision nuances are abstracted to boolean predicates.
risks: Resource contention: exclusive locking of evidence or check resources may create bottlenecks under load.
False positive escalation: legitimate transactions may be escalated causing operational overhead and customer friction.
Single point approval gating: reliance on a limited approver token can delay case closure if approver unavailable.
State inconsistency if tokens are not properly released due to aborted workflows or exceptional paths.
